variant.handshake_hint: Use noise NN (no PSK, no auth) — simplest noise for comparison.
variant.padding_hint: Use mimicry/video-streaming to test a different length distribution.

generation_result.reasoning: We use TLS transport with Chrome 120 fingerprint and SNI set to cdn.jsdelivr.net to evade M1-M3. Noise NN handshake is used as requested, without PSK, for simplicity and to avoid static keys. Padding is set to mimic video-streaming traffic to vary packet sizes and avoid uniform size detection (M4, M6). Fallback reverse-proxy ensures active probes (M8) return valid HTTPS responses. Timing is set to none to avoid artificial patterns (M7). The main weakness is the potential fixed-size initial handshake records under TLS, which might be atypical for video streaming and could be flagged by M5.
known_weaknesses: The initial application data records for the Noise NN handshake (two fixed-length messages) might be detected by sequence-based classifiers (M5) because video streaming typically has variable early packet sizes.
